name: unstructured-inference

metadata.name: unstructured-inference
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: A library for performing inference using trained models.

documentation:
Open-Source Pre-Processing Tools for Unstructured Data

The `unstructured-inference` repo contains hosted model inference code for layout parsing models. These models are invoked via API as part of the partitioning bricks in the `unstructured` package. ## Installation ### Package Run `pip install unstructured-inference`. ### Detectron2 [Detectron2](https://github.com/facebookresearch/detectron2) is required for using models from the [layoutparser model zoo](#using-models-from-the-layoutparser-model-zoo) but is not automatically installed with this package. For MacOS and Linux, build from source with: ```shell pip install 'git+https://github.com/facebookresearch/detectron2.git@57bdb21249d5418c130d54e2ebdc94dda7a4c01a' ``` Other install options can be found in the [Detectron2 installation guide](https://detectron2.readthedocs.io/en/latest/tutorials/install.html). Windows is not officially supported by Detectron2, but some users are able to install it anyway. See discussion [here](https://layout-parser.github.io/tutorials/installation#for-windows-users) for tips on installing Detectron2 on Windows. ### Repository To install the repository for development, clone the repo and run `make install` to install dependencies. Run `make help` for a full list of install options. ## Getting Started To get started with the layout parsing model, use the following commands: ```python from unstructured_inference.inference.layout import DocumentLayout layout = DocumentLayout.from_file("sample-docs/loremipsum.pdf") print(layout.pages[0].elements) ``` Once the model has detected the layout and OCR'd the document, the text extracted from the first page of the sample document will be displayed. You can convert a given element to a `dict` by running the `.to_dict()` method. ## Models The inference pipeline operates by finding text elements in a document page using a detection model, then extracting the contents of the elements using direct extraction (if available), OCR, and optionally table inference models. We offer several detection models including [Detectron2](https://github.com/facebookresearch/detectron2) and [YOLOX](https://github.com/Megvii-BaseDetection/YOLOX). ### Using a non-default model When doing inference, an alternate model can be used by passing the model object to the ingestion method via the `model` parameter. The `get_model` function can be used to construct one of our out-of-the-box models from a keyword, e.g.: ```python from unstructured_inference.models.base import get_model from unstructured_inference.inference.layout import DocumentLayout model = get_model("yolox") layout = DocumentLayout.from_file("sample-docs/layout-parser-paper.pdf", detection_model=model) ``` ### Using models from the layoutparser model zoo The `UnstructuredDetectronModel` class in `unstructured_inference.modelts.detectron2` uses the `faster_rcnn_R_50_FPN_3x` model pretrained on DocLayNet, but by using different construction parameters, any model in the `layoutparser` [model zoo](https://layout-parser.readthedocs.io/en/latest/notes/modelzoo.html) can be used. `UnstructuredDetectronModel` is a light wrapper around the `layoutparser` `Detectron2LayoutModel` object, and accepts the same arguments. See [layoutparser documentation](https://layout-parser.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api_doc/models.html#layoutparser.models.Detectron2LayoutModel) for details. ### Using your own model Any detection model can be used for in the `unstructured_inference` pipeline by wrapping the model in the `UnstructuredObjectDetectionModel` class. To integrate with the `DocumentLayout` class, a subclass of `UnstructuredObjectDetectionModel` must have a `predict` method that accepts a `PIL.Image.Image` and returns a list of `LayoutElement`s, and an `initialize` method, which loads the model and prepares it for inference. ## Security Policy See our [security policy](https://github.com/Unstructured-IO/unstructured-inference/security/policy) for information on how to report security vulnerabilities. ## Learn more | Section | Description | |-|-| | [Unstructured Community Github](https://github.com/Unstructured-IO/community) | Information about Unstructured.io community projects | | [Unstructured Github](https://github.com/Unstructured-IO) | Unstructured.io open source repositories | | [Company Website](https://unstructured.io) | Unstructured.io product and company info |

Slot a collection of objects into the container they occupy most (the container which holds the

Remove any objects (these can be rows, columns, supercells, etc.) that don't

Reduce the set of spans to those that fall within a bounding box.

threshold: the fraction of the span that must overlap with the bbox.

documentation: Test if more than "threshold" fraction of bbox1 overlaps with bbox2.

documentation: Put the objects in order from left to right.

functions.name: unstructured_inference.models.table_postprocess.sort_objects_top_to_bottom
documentation: Put the objects in order from top to bottom.

functions.name: unstructured_inference.models.table_postprocess.align_columns
documentation:
For every column, align the top and bottom boundaries to the final
table bounding box.

functions.name: unstructured_inference.models.table_postprocess.align_rows
documentation:
For every row, align the left and right boundaries to the final
table bounding box.

functions.name: unstructured_inference.models.table_postprocess.nms
documentation:
A customizable version of non-maxima suppression (NMS).

Default behavior: If a lower-confidence object overlaps more than 5% of its area
with a higher-confidence object, remove the lower-confidence object.

objects: set of dicts; each object dict must have a 'bbox' and a 'score' field
match_criteria: how to measure how much two objects "overlap"
match_threshold: the cutoff for determining that overlap requires suppression of one object
keep_higher: if True, keep the object with the higher metric; otherwise, keep the lower

functions.name: unstructured_inference.models.table_postprocess.align_supercells
documentation:
For each supercell, align it to the rows it intersects 50% of the height of,
and the columns it intersects 50% of the width of.
Eliminate supercells for which there are no rows and columns it intersects 50% with.

functions.name: unstructured_inference.models.table_postprocess.nms_supercells
documentation:
A NMS scheme for supercells that first attempts to shrink supercells to
resolve overlap.
If two supercells overlap the same (sub)cell, shrink the lower confidence
supercell to resolve the overlap. If shrunk supercell is empty, remove it.

functions.name: unstructured_inference.models.table_postprocess.header_supercell_tree
documentation:
Make sure no supercell in the header is below more than one supercell in any row above it.
The cells in the header form a tree, but a supercell with more than one supercell in a row
above it means that some cell has more than one parent, which is not allowed. Eliminate
any supercell that would cause this to be violated.

functions.name: unstructured_inference.models.table_postprocess.remove_supercell_overlap
documentation:
This function resolves overlap between supercells (supercells must be
disjoint) by iteratively shrinking supercells by the fewest grid cells
necessary to resolve the overlap.
Example:
If two supercells overlap at grid cell (R, C), and supercell #1 is less
confident than supercell #2, we eliminate either row R from supercell #1
or column C from supercell #1 by comparing the number of columns in row R
versus the number of rows in column C. If the number of columns in row R
is less than the number of rows in column C, we eliminate row R from
supercell #1. This resolves the overlap by removing fewer grid cells from
supercell #1 than if we eliminated column C from it.

documentation: a simple tokenizer that splits text by white space

functions.name: unstructured_inference.models.eval.compare_contents_as_df
documentation:
ravel the table as string then use text distance to compare the prediction against true
table

Parameters
----------
actual_df: pd.DataFrame
    actual table as pandas dataframe

pred_df: pd.DataFrame
    predicted table as pandas dataframe

eval_func: str, default tp "token_ratio"
    the eval_func should be one of "token_ratio", "ratio", "partial_token_ratio",
    "partial_ratio". Those are functions provided by rapidfuzz to evaluate text distances
    using either tokens or characters. In general token is better than characters for evaluating
    tables.

processor: Callable, default to None
    processor to tokenize the text; by default None means no processing (using characters). For
    tokens eval functions we recommend using the `default_tokenizer` or some other functions to
    break down the text into words

tab_token: str, default to "\t"
    the string to join cells together

row_break_token: str, default to "\n"
    the string to join rows together

Returns
-------
Dict[str, int]
    mapping of by column and by row scores to the scores as float numbers

classes.name: unstructured_inference.utils.LazyEvaluateInfo
documentation:
Class that stores the information needed to lazily evaluate a function with given arguments.
The object stores the information needed for evaluation as a function and its arguments.

classes.name: unstructured_inference.utils.LazyDict
documentation:
Class that wraps a dict and only evaluates keys of the dict when the key is accessed. Keys
that should be evaluated lazily should use LazyEvaluateInfo objects as values. By default when
a value is computed from a LazyEvaluateInfo object, it is converted to the raw value in the
internal dict, so subsequent accessing of the key will produce the same value. Set cache=False
to avoid storing the raw value.

Unstructured modified the original source code found at

functions.name: unstructured_inference.visualize.draw_bbox
documentation: Draws bounding box in image

functions.name: unstructured_inference.visualize.show_plot
documentation:
Display an image using matplotlib with an optional desired width while maintaining the aspect
 ratio.

Parameters:
- image (Union[Image, np.ndarray]): An image in PIL Image format or a numpy ndarray format.
- desired_width (Optional[int]): Desired width for the display size of the image.
    If provided, the height is calculated based on the original aspect ratio.
    If not provided, the image will be displayed with its original dimensions.

Raises:
- ValueError: If the provided image type is neither PIL Image nor numpy ndarray.

Returns:
- None: The function displays the image using matplotlib but does not return any value.

functions.name: unstructured_inference.logger.translate_log_level
documentation:
Translate Python debugg level to ONNX runtime error level
since blank pages error are shown at level 3 that should be the
exception, and 4 the normal behavior

functions.name: unstructured_inference.models.tables.apply_thresholds_on_objects
documentation:
Filters predicted objects which the confidence scores below the thresholds

Args:
    objects: Sequence of mappings for example:
    [
        {
            "label": "table row",
            "score": 0.55,
            "bbox": [...],
        },
        ...,
    ]
    thresholds: Mapping from labels to thresholds

Returns:
    Filtered list of objects

functions.name: unstructured_inference.models.tables.box_cxcywh_to_xyxy
documentation:
Convert rectangle format from center-x, center-y, width, height to
x-min, y-min, x-max, y-max.
for output bounding box post-processing

functions.name: unstructured_inference.models.tables.rescale_bboxes
documentation: Rescale relative bounding box to box of size given by size.

functions.name: unstructured_inference.models.tables.iob
documentation: Compute the intersection area over box area, for bbox1.

functions.name: unstructured_inference.models.tables.objects_to_structures
documentation:
Process the bounding boxes produced by the table structure recognition model into
a *consistent* set of table structures (rows, columns, spanning cells, headers).
This entails resolving conflicts/overlaps, and ensuring the boxes meet certain alignment
conditions (for example: rows should all have the same width, etc.).

functions.name: unstructured_inference.models.tables.refine_table_structure
documentation:
Apply operations to the detected table structure objects such as
thresholding, NMS, and alignment.

functions.name: unstructured_inference.models.tables.align_headers
documentation:
Adjust the header boundary to be the convex hull of the rows it intersects
at least 50% of the height of.

For now, we are not supporting tables with multiple headers, so we need to
eliminate anything besides the top-most header.

functions.name: unstructured_inference.models.tables.compute_confidence_score
documentation:
Compute a confidence score based on how well the page tokens
slot into the cells reported by the model

functions.name: unstructured_inference.models.tables.structure_to_cells
documentation:
Assuming the row, column, spanning cell, and header bounding boxes have
been refined into a set of consistent table structures, process these
table structures into table cells. This is a universal representation
format for the table, which can later be exported to Pandas or CSV formats.
Classify the cells as header/access cells or data cells
based on if they intersect with the header bounding box.

functions.name: unstructured_inference.models.tables.fill_cells
documentation:
fills the missing cells in the table by adding a cells with empty text
where there are no cells detected by the model.

A cell contains the following keys relevent to the html conversion:
row_nums: List[int]
    the row numbers this cell belongs to; for cells spanning multiple rows there are more than
    one numbers
column_nums: List[int]
    the columns numbers this cell belongs to; for cells spanning multiple columns there are more
    than one numbers
cell text: str
    the text in this cell
column header: bool
    whether this cell is a column header

functions.name: unstructured_inference.models.tables.cells_to_html
documentation:
Convert table structure to html format.

Args:
    cells: List of dictionaries representing table cells, where each dictionary has the
        following format:
        {
            "row_nums": List[int],
            "column_nums": List[int],
            "cell text": str,
            "column header": bool,
        }
Returns:
    str: HTML table string

functions.name: unstructured_inference.models.tables.zoom_image
documentation:
scale an image based on the zoom factor using cv2; the scaled image is post processed by
dilation then erosion to improve edge sharpness for OCR tasks

classes.name: unstructured_inference.models.tables.UnstructuredTableTransformerModel

methods.name: unstructured_inference.models.tables.UnstructuredTableTransformerModel.predict
documentation:
Predict table structure deferring to run_prediction with ocr tokens

Note:
`ocr_tokens` is a list of dictionaries representing OCR tokens,
where each dictionary has the following format:
{
    "bbox": [int, int, int, int],  # Bounding box coordinates of the token
    "block_num": int,  # Block number
    "line_num": int,   # Line number
    "span_num": int,   # Span number
    "text": str,  # Text content of the token
}
The bounding box coordinates should match the table structure.
FIXME: refactor token data into a dataclass so we have clear expectations of the fields

Annotates the elements on the page image.

sources is a list of sources to annotate. If sources is ["all"], then all sources will be
annotated. Current sources allowed are "yolox","detectron2_onnx" and "detectron2_lp"

modules.name: unstructured_inference.config
documentation:
This module contains variables that can permitted to be tweaked by the system environment. For
example, model parameters that changes the output of an inference call. Constants do NOT belong in
this module. Constants are values that are usually names for common options (e.g., color names) or
settings that should not be altered without making a code change (e.g., definition of 1Gb of memory
in bytes). Constants should go into `./constants.py`

classes.name: unstructured_inference.config.InferenceConfig

methods.name: unstructured_inference.config.InferenceConfig._get_string
documentation:
attempt to get the value of var from the os environment; if not present return the
default_value

methods.name: unstructured_inference.config.InferenceConfig.TABLE_IMAGE_BACKGROUND_PAD
documentation:
number of pixels to pad around an table image with a white background color

The padding adds NO image data around an identified table bounding box; it simply adds white
background around the image

methods.name: unstructured_inference.config.InferenceConfig.TT_TABLE_CONF
documentation: confidence threshold for table identified by table transformer

methods.name: unstructured_inference.config.InferenceConfig.TABLE_COLUMN_CONF
documentation: confidence threshold for column identified by table transformer

methods.name: unstructured_inference.config.InferenceConfig.TABLE_ROW_CONF
documentation: confidence threshold for column identified by table transformer

methods.name: unstructured_inference.config.InferenceConfig.TABLE_COLUMN_HEADER_CONF
documentation: confidence threshold for column header identified by table transformer

methods.name: unstructured_inference.config.InferenceConfig.TABLE_PROJECTED_ROW_HEADER_CONF
documentation: confidence threshold for projected row header identified by table transformer

methods.name: unstructured_inference.config.InferenceConfig.TABLE_SPANNING_CELL_CONF
documentation: confidence threshold for table spanning cells identified by table transformer

methods.name: unstructured_inference.config.InferenceConfig.TABLE_IOB_THRESHOLD
documentation:
minimum intersection over box area ratio for a box to be considered part of a larger box
it intersects

methods.name: unstructured_inference.config.InferenceConfig.LAYOUT_SAME_REGION_THRESHOLD
documentation:
threshold for two layouts' bounding boxes to be considered as the same region

When the intersection area over union area of the two is larger than this threshold the two
boxes are considered the same region

methods.name: unstructured_inference.config.InferenceConfig.LAYOUT_SUBREGION_THRESHOLD
documentation:
threshold for one bounding box to be considered as a sub-region of another bounding box

When the intersection region area divided by self area is larger than this threshold self is
considered a subregion of the other

methods.name: unstructured_inference.config.InferenceConfig.ELEMENTS_H_PADDING_COEF
documentation:
When extending the boundaries of a PDF object for the purpose of determining which other
elements should be considered in the same text region, we use a relative distance based on
some fraction of the block height (typically character height). This is the fraction used
for the horizontal extension applied to the left and right sides.

methods.name: unstructured_inference.config.InferenceConfig.ELEMENTS_V_PADDING_COEF
documentation: Same as ELEMENTS_H_PADDING_COEF but the vertical extension.
class for configuring inference parameters

functions.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.minimal_containing_region
documentation: Returns the smallest rectangular region that contains all regions passed

functions.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.intersections
documentation:
Returns a square boolean matrix of intersections of an arbitrary number of rectangles, i.e.
the ijth entry of the matrix is True if and only if the ith Rectangle and jth Rectangle
intersect.

functions.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.coords_intersections
documentation:
Returns a square boolean matrix of intersections of given stack of coords, i.e.
the ijth entry of the matrix is True if and only if the ith coords and jth coords
intersect.

functions.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.region_bounding_boxes_are_almost_the_same
documentation:
Returns whether bounding boxes are almost the same. This is determined by checking if the
intersection over union is above some threshold.

functions.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.grow_region_to_match_region
documentation: Grows a region to the minimum size necessary to contain both regions.

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.Rectangle.pad
documentation:
Increases (or decreases, if padding is negative) the size of the rectangle by extending
the boundary outward (resp. inward).

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.Rectangle.hpad
documentation:
Increases (or decreases, if padding is negative) the size of the rectangle by extending
the left and right sides of the boundary outward (resp. inward).

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.Rectangle.vpad
documentation:
Increases (or decreases, if padding is negative) the size of the rectangle by extending
the top and bottom of the boundary outward (resp. inward).

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.Rectangle.width
documentation: Width of rectangle

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.Rectangle.height
documentation: Height of rectangle

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.Rectangle.x_midpoint
documentation: Finds the horizontal midpoint of the object.

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.Rectangle.y_midpoint
documentation: Finds the vertical midpoint of the object.

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.Rectangle.is_disjoint
documentation: Checks whether this rectangle is disjoint from another rectangle.

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.Rectangle.intersects
documentation: Checks whether this rectangle intersects another rectangle.

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.Rectangle.is_in
documentation: Checks whether this rectangle is contained within another rectangle.

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.Rectangle._has_none
documentation: return false when one of the coord is nan

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.Rectangle.coordinates
documentation: Gets coordinates of the rectangle

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.Rectangle.intersection
documentation:
Gives the rectangle that is the intersection of two rectangles, or None if the
rectangles are disjoint.

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.Rectangle.area
documentation: Gives the area of the rectangle.

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.Rectangle.intersection_over_union
documentation:
Gives the intersection-over-union of two rectangles. This tends to be a good metric of
how similar the regions are. Returns 0 for disjoint rectangles, 1 for two identical
rectangles -- area of intersection / area of union.

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.Rectangle.intersection_over_minimum
documentation:
Gives the area-of-intersection over the minimum of the areas of the rectangles. Useful
for identifying when one rectangle is almost-a-subset of the other. Returns 0 for disjoint
rectangles, 1 when either is a subset of the other.

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.Rectangle.is_almost_subregion_of
documentation:
Returns whether this region is almost a subregion of other. This is determined by
comparing the intersection area over self area to some threshold, and checking whether self
is the smaller rectangle.

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.TextRegion.from_coords
documentation: Constructs a region from coordinates.

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.TextRegions.slice
documentation: slice text regions based on indices

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.TextRegions.as_list
documentation: return a list of TextRegion objects representing the data

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.TextRegions.from_list
documentation:
create TextRegions from a list of TextRegion objects; the objects must have the same
source

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.TextRegions.x1
documentation: left coordinate

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.TextRegions.y1
documentation: top coordinate

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.TextRegions.x2
documentation: right coordinate

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.TextRegions.y2
documentation: bottom coordinate

methods.name: unstructured_inference.inference.elements.TextRegions.areas
documentation: areas of each region; only compute it when it is needed

modules.name: unstructured_inference.math
documentation: a lightweight module that provides helpers to common math operations

functions.name: unstructured_inference.math.safe_division
documentation:
a safer division to avoid division by zero when b == 0

returns a/b or a/FLOAT_EPSILON (should be around 2.2E-16) when b == 0

Parameters:
- a (int/float): a in a/b
- b (int/float): b in a/b

Returns:
float: a/b or a/FLOAT_EPSILON (should be around 2.2E-16) when b == 0

functions.name: unstructured_inference.inference.layoutelement.merge_inferred_layout_with_extracted_layout
documentation: Merge two layouts to produce a single layout.

functions.name: unstructured_inference.inference.layoutelement.separate

functions.name: unstructured_inference.inference.layoutelement.table_cells_to_dataframe

functions.name: unstructured_inference.inference.layoutelement.intersection_areas_between_coords

After this function, the list of elements will not contain any element inside
